title: ophrys aranifera subsp. parnassica
intro: Ophrys aranifera subsp. parnassica is a rare orchid species endemic to Greece, belonging to the genus Ophrys, commonly known as the "bee orchid." This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Ophrys aranifera subsp. parnassica include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions or decoctions, and it can also be dried and used as a tea, although proper dosing and preparation are essential to maximize its potential benefits.
